---
title: "Your Document Title"
author: "Jacopo Olivieri"
date: "2023-02-22"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_width: 6
    fig_aspect_ratio: 0.618
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    echo: false
    warning: false
    message: false
    fig.align: "center"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Initialize packages with version control
if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("renv")
}

# Run manual renv commands if needed
# renv::activate() # Activate the project library
# renv::restore()  # Restore the environment if running for first time
# renv::snapshot()  # After adding/updating packages, snapshot the state


# Load required packages
required_packages <- c("rmarkdown", "tidyverse", "fs", "stringr", "here", "utils", "logger")
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  new_packages <- packages[!sapply(packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]
  if (length(new_packages) > 0) {
    install.packages(new_packages)
  }
  invisible(sapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
}
install_if_missing(required_packages)


# Initialize logging
log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "test_unmatched.log")
if (!dir.exists(dirname(log_path))) {
  dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE)
}
logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
```

## Load Data

```{r}
data <- import(here("data", "processed", "merged_edm_data.RData"), trust = TRUE)
data_summary <- import(here("data", "processed", "merged_edm_data_with_summary.RData"), trust = TRUE)
annual_data <- import(here("data","processed", "annual_return_edm.rds"),
                      trust = TRUE) 
```


```{r}
data_summary$summary
data_summary$data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water",
         year == 2022,
         merge_quality == "no_match") %>% 
  pull(site_name_ea) %>% unique()
```


### Fixing South West Water 2022 and 2023

```{r}
individ_data <- import(here("data","processed", "combined_edm_data.RData"),
                       trust = TRUE) 

# 2022 is problematic
leftsouth22 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2022) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

rightsouth22 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2022) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

left_join(leftsouth22, rightsouth22) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))

# 2023
leftsouth23 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2023) %>% 
   select(where(~!all(is.na(.)))) %>% 
  select(-contains("site"))

rightsouth23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "South West Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)
rightsouth23$u

# this fixes 2023
left_join(leftsouth23, rightsouth23) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))
```



### Fixing Yorkshire Water 2022

```{r}
individ_data <- import(here("data","processed", "combined_edm_data.RData"),
                       trust = TRUE) 

york22 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water", year == 2022) %>% 
  mutate(unique_id = paste0("YW", unique_id))

york23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Yorkshire Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)

left_join(york22, york23, by = join_by(water_company, unique_id)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr)) %>% 
  select(water_company, unique_id, outlet_discharge_ngr)
```


### Fixing Thames Water 2023

```{r}
leftthames23 <- individ_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Thames Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))  %>% 
  mutate(across(
    where(is.character) & matches("site"), tolower))

rightthames23 <- annual_data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Thames Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))  %>% 
  mutate(across(
    where(is.character) & matches("site"), tolower))

left_join(leftthames23, rightthames23,
          by = join_by(year, site_name_wa_sc, unique_id),
          suffix = c("", "_annual")) %>% 
  filter(is.na(outlet_discharge_ngr))
  
rightthames23 %>% 
  mutate(across(where(is.character), tolower))
```


### Fixing merge key

```{r}
data <- data_summary$data
dat <- load_data()

water_companies <- unique(data$water_company)

for (i in seq_along(water_companies)) {
  company <- water_companies[i]
  print(company)
  
  join <- data %>% 
  filter(water_company == company, year == 2023) %>% 
  pull(key_join) %>% 
  unique()
  print(join)

}



current_company <- "Southern Water"
current_year <- 2023
      
# Get individual and annual spill data for current company-year
# Remove columns that are all NA as they may differ between datasets
indiv <- dat$individual %>%
filter(water_company == current_company, year == current_year) %>%
select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

annual <- annual_data %>%
  filter(water_company == current_company, 
         year == current_year, 
         !is.na(counted_spills)) %>%
  select(where(~!all(is.na(.))))

# Define joining keys: common columns plus location identifier
# In 2023, we have unique id, so we use that as the join key
cols_annual_dat <- c("outlet_discharge_ngr")
key_join <- intersect(names(indiv), names(annual))
if (current_year == 2023 & "unique_id" %in% key_join) {
  key_join <- c("water_company", "year", "unique_id")
}
key_cols <- c(key_join, cols_annual_dat)




data %>% 
  filter(water_company == "Southern Water", year == 2023) %>% 
  pull(key_join) %>% 
  unique()

data %>% 
  filter(year == 2023) 

unique(data$key_join)
grepl("unique_id", unique(data$key_join))
unique(data$key_join)[grepl("unique_id", unique(data$key_join))]

x <- unique(data$key_join)[[3]]
x <- unique(data$key_join)[[1]]

x
if ("unique_id" %in% x) {
  y = x[x!="unique_id"]
} else {
  y = x
}
y

subset(x, x!="unique_id")

x[x!="unique_id"]
```

```{r}
x <- import(here("data", "processed", "combined_edm_data.parquet")) %>% 
  as_tibble() %>% 
  arrange(water_company)

combined_df <- combined_df %>% 
  arrange(water_company)

names(x)
all.equal(x$water_company, combined_df$water_company)

tibble(x = x$water_company, y = combined_df$water_company) %>% 
  filter(x!=y)

```


```{r}
############################################################
# Create Unique Spill Sites
# Project: Sewage
# Date: 03/02/2025
# Author: Jacopo Olivieri
############################################################

#' This script creates unique spill sites from the merged EDM data
#' by cleaning location data and extracting distinct sites.

# Set Up Functions
############################################################

#' Initialize the R environment with required packages and settings
#' @return NULL
initialise_environment <- function() {
    if (!requireNamespace("renv", quietly = TRUE)) {
        install.packages("renv")
        renv::init()
    }

    required_packages <- c(
        "rmarkdown", "rio", "tidyverse", "purrr", "here", "logger", "glue", 
        "fs", "rnrfa", "arrow"
    )

    # Install and load packages
    invisible(sapply(required_packages, function(pkg) {
        if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
            install.packages(pkg)
        }
        library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
    }))
}

#' Set up logging configuration
#' @return NULL
setup_logging <- function() {
    log_path <- here::here("output", "log", "13_create_unique_spill_sites.log")
    dir.create(dirname(log_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

    logger::log_appender(logger::appender_file(log_path))
    logger::log_layout(logger::layout_glue_colors)
    logger::log_threshold(logger::DEBUG)
    logger::log_info("Script started at {Sys.time()}")
}


# Configuration
############################################################

CONFIG <- list(
    spill_data_path = here::here(
      "data", "processed", "matched_events_annual_data", 
      "matched_events_annual_data.parquet"),
    unique_spills_parquet = here::here(
      "data", "processed", "matched_events_annual_data", 
      "unique_spill_sites.parquet"),
    unique_spills_xlsx = here::here(
      "data", "processed", "matched_events_annual_data", 
      "unique_spill_sites.xlsx")
)


# Functions
############################################################

#' Load individual sewage spill data
#' @return A data frame containing individual sewage spill records
load_data <- function() {
    file_path <- fs::path(CONFIG$spill_data_path)
    logger::log_info("Loading data: {file_path}")

    if (!file.exists(file_path)) {
        stop(glue::glue("File not found: {file_path}"))
    }

    tryCatch(
        {
            df <- rio::import(file_path, trust = TRUE)
            logger::log_info("Loaded data")
            return(df)
        },
        error = function(e) {
            error_msg <- glue::glue("Failed to load data: {e$message}")
            logger::log_error(error_msg)
            stop(error_msg)
        }
    )
}


#' Standardize grid references (NGR)
#' @param x Character vector of raw NGR strings
#' @return Cleaned NGR strings or NA
clean_ngr <- function(x) {
  x <- dplyr::case_when(
    toupper(x) %in% c("UNABLE TO MATCH TO CONSENTS DATABASE",
                     "NOT IN CONSENTS DATABASE") ~ NA_character_,
    TRUE ~ x
  )

  # Extract content after 'and', drop trailing notes
  x <- ifelse(stringr::str_detect(x, "(?i)and"),
              stringr::str_extract(x, "(?i)(?<=and).*"), x)
  x <- stringr::str_replace(x, "(?i)Not.*", "")

  # Keep only leading alphanumeric block
  x <- stringr::str_extract(x, "^[^,&/\\(]+")
  x <- stringr::str_remove_all(x, "\\s+")

  trimws(x)
}


#' Derive unique spill sites with OSGB coordinates
#' @param data Raw spill dataframe
#' @return Tidy table: one row per site, availability per year, BNG coords
clean_location <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    # clean ngr
    mutate(ngr_clean = clean_ngr(ngr)) %>% 
    # deduplicate on cleaned ref
    distinct(water_company, year, site_id, ngr_clean) %>%
    # pivot availability by year
    mutate(available = TRUE) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      id_cols     = c(water_company, site_id, ngr_clean),
      names_from  = year,
      values_from = available,
      names_prefix= "available_year_",
      values_fill = FALSE
    ) %>%
    # parse British National Grid coords
    mutate(
      coords = map(ngr_clean, ~ tryCatch(
        rnrfa::osg_parse(.x, coord_system = "BNG"),
        error = function(e) list(easting=NA_real_, northing=NA_real_)
      ))
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      easting  = map_dbl(coords, "easting"),
      northing = map_dbl(coords, "northing")
    ) %>%
    select(-coords)
}


#' Export unique spills data to parquet and Excel
#' @param unique_spills_df The unique spills dataframe
#' @param excel_output Path to Excel output file
#' @param parquet_output Path to parquet output file
#' @return NULL
export_data <- function(
    unique_spills_df,
    excel_output = CONFIG$unique_spills_xlsx,
    parquet_output = CONFIG$unique_spills_parquet) {
  tryCatch(
    {

      # Export to parquet files
      arrow::write_parquet(unique_spills_df, parquet_output)
      logger::log_info("Exported to parquet: {parquet_output}")

      # Export to Excel 
      rio::export(unique_spills_df, excel_output)
      logger::log_info("Exported data to Excel file: {excel_output}")
    },
    error = function(e) {
      logger::log_error("Data export failed: {e$message}")
      stop(glue::glue("Failed to export data: {e$message}"))
    }
  )
}

# Main execution
############################################################

main <- function() {
    tryCatch(
        {
          # Setup
          initialise_environment()
          setup_logging()

          # Load and process data
          logger::log_info("Loading processed merged data")
          spill_data <- load_data()

          logger::log_info("Creating unique spill sites")
          unique_sites <- spill_data %>%
            clean_location() %>%
            relocate(site_id, everything())

          # Export results
          logger::log_info("Exporting unique spill sites")
          export_data(unique_spills_df = unique_sites)
          logger::log_info("Processing completed successfully")
        },
        error = function(e) {
            logger::log_error("Fatal error: {e$message}")
            stop(e)
        }
    )
}

# Execute main function if script is run directly
if (sys.nframe() == 0) {
    main()
}

```

```{r}
df <- import("/Users/jacopoolivieri/Downloads/test.csv")
```

```{r}
# Clean column names
df_clean <- df %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Filter data to keep only the 'Negative' (baseline) and 'Negative + Negative x Treated' (combined effect) rows
df_filtered <- df_clean %>%
  filter(coefficient %in% c("Negative", "Negative + Negative x Treated"))

# Reshape the data to long format
df_long <- df_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("est_") | starts_with("se_"),
    names_to = c(".value", "condition"),
    names_pattern = "(est|se)_(.*)"
  ) %>%
  # Improve readability of condition names
  mutate(condition = case_when(
    condition == "below_p50" ~ "Below p50",
    condition == "above_p50" ~ "Above p50",
    condition == "all_shocks" ~ "All Shocks",
    TRUE ~ condition
  )) %>%
  # Convert 'condition' to a factor for ordering and colour mapping
  mutate(condition = factor(condition, levels = c("Below p50", "Above p50", "All Shocks"))) %>%
  # Improve readability of coefficient names for the x-axis
   mutate(coefficient_label = case_when(
     coefficient == "Negative" ~ "Baseline\n(Shock Only)", # Using \n for line break
     coefficient == "Negative + Negative x Treated" ~ "Combined\n(Shock + Treatment)", # Using \n for line break
     TRUE ~ coefficient
   )) %>%
   # Convert 'coefficient_label' to a factor to control x-axis order
   mutate(coefficient_label = factor(coefficient_label, levels = c("Baseline\n(Shock Only)", "Combined\n(Shock + Treatment)"))) %>%
   # Shorten variable names for better facet titles
   mutate(variable_short = str_replace(variable, " Consumpti", " Cons.") %>%
                           str_replace(" Intake", " Intake")) %>%
   # Convert 'variable_short' to factor for facet ordering (optional, alphabetical by default)
   mutate(variable_short = factor(variable_short))


# --- Plotting ---

# Define the amount of dodge
dodge_width <- 0.4 # Adjust this value to control spacing

# Create the ggplot object with updated aesthetics and faceting
ggplot(
    df_long,
    # Define aesthetics:
    # x-axis: effect type ('coefficient_label': Baseline vs Combined)
    # y-axis: estimate value ('est')
    # color: distinguish lines/points based on condition ('condition')
    aes(x = coefficient_label, y = est, color = condition)
  ) +
  # Add lines connecting the baseline point to the combined effect point for each condition within each facet
  # 'group = condition' ensures lines are drawn connecting the two points for the same condition
  # 'position = position_dodge()' moves the lines side-by-side based on 'color'
  geom_line(aes(group = condition), position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add points representing the specific point estimates
  # 'position = position_dodge()' moves the points side-by-side
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), size = 3.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  # Add vertical error bars representing standard error (Estimate +/- SE)
  # 'position = position_dodge()' moves the error bars side-by-side
  # 'width' controls the horizontal size of the error bar caps (make it smaller than dodge_width)
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = est - se, ymax = est + se),
    position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
    width = 0.1, # Width of the error bar caps
    linewidth = 0.7
   ) +
  # Create separate panels (facets) for each outcome variable ('variable_short')
  # 'ncol = 3' arranges the facets horizontally
  facet_wrap(~ variable_short, ncol = 3) +
  # Add a horizontal dashed line at y=0 for reference
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.8) +
  # Customize labels and title
  labs(
    title = "Impact of Treatment during Negative Shocks by Outcome Variable",
    subtitle = "Comparing Baseline (Shock Only) vs. Combined Effect (Shock + Treatment)",
    x = "Effect Type", # Updated x-axis label
    y = "Estimated Coefficient", # Updated y-axis label
    color = "Shock Type" # Updated legend title
  ) +
  # Apply a minimal theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  # Further theme customization
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")
  ) +
  # Use a colorblind-friendly color palette for the conditions
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Or "Dark2", "Paired"

# Display the plot
print(plot_vertical_dodged)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
# Ensure you have these installed: install.packages(c("tidyverse", "janitor", "stringr"))
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(stringr)

# --- Data Preparation ---

# Recreate the data provided by the user
# In a real scenario, you would load this from a file, e.g., using read_csv("your_data_file.csv")
data_text <- "
Coefficient,Variable,Est below p50,SE below p50,Est above p50,SE above p50,Est all shocks,SE all shocks
Negative,Total Consumpti,-0.2,0.1,-0.6,0.1,-0.42,0.1
Negative x Treated,Total Consumpti,0.3,0.1,0.4,0.1,0.44,0.1
Negative + Negative x Treated,Total Consumpti,0.1,0.1,-0.2,0.1,0.02,0.1
Negative,Food Consumpti,-0.15,0.1,-0.55,0.1,-0.34,0.1
Negative x Treated,Food Consumpti,0.25,0.1,0.35,0.1,0.35,0.1
Negative + Negative x Treated,Food Consumpti,0.1,0.1,-0.2,0.1,0.01,0.1
Negative,Protein Intake,-0.25,0.1,-0.7,0.1,-0.3,0.1
Negative x Treated,Protein Intake,0.25,0.1,0.3,0.1,0.32,0.1
Negative + Negative x Treated,Protein Intake,0,0.1,-0.4,0.1,0.02,0.1
"

# Read the data from the text string
df <- read_csv(data_text, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Clean column names
df_clean <- df %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Filter data to keep only the 'Negative' (baseline) and 'Negative + Negative x Treated' (combined effect) rows
df_filtered <- df_clean %>%
  filter(coefficient %in% c("Negative", "Negative + Negative x Treated"))

# Reshape the data to long format
df_long <- df_filtered %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("est_") | starts_with("se_"),
    names_to = c(".value", "condition"),
    names_pattern = "(est|se)_(.*)"
  ) %>%
  # Improve readability of condition names
  mutate(condition_label = case_when( # Renamed to avoid conflict before filtering
    condition == "below_p50" ~ "Below p50",
    condition == "above_p50" ~ "Above p50",
    condition == "all_shocks" ~ "All Shocks",
    TRUE ~ condition
  )) %>%
  # *** ADDED FILTER: Remove 'All Shocks' condition ***
  filter(condition_label != "All Shocks") %>%
  # Convert 'condition_label' to a factor for ordering and colour mapping
  # *** UPDATED LEVELS: Removed 'All Shocks' from factor levels ***
  mutate(condition = factor(condition_label, levels = c("Below p50", "Above p50"))) %>%
  # Improve readability of coefficient names for the x-axis
   mutate(coefficient_label = case_when(
     coefficient == "Negative" ~ "Baseline\n(Shock Only)", # Using \n for line break
     coefficient == "Negative + Negative x Treated" ~ "Combined\n(Shock + Treatment)", # Using \n for line break
     TRUE ~ coefficient
   )) %>%
   # Convert 'coefficient_label' to a factor to control x-axis order
   mutate(coefficient_label = factor(coefficient_label, levels = c("Baseline\n(Shock Only)", "Combined\n(Shock + Treatment)"))) %>%
   # Shorten variable names for better facet titles
   mutate(variable_short = str_replace(variable, " Consumpti", " Cons.") %>%
                           str_replace(" Intake", " Intake")) %>%
   # Convert 'variable_short' to factor for facet ordering (optional, alphabetical by default)
   mutate(variable_short = factor(variable_short))


# --- Plotting ---

# Define the amount of dodge
dodge_width <- 0.4 # Adjust this value to control spacing

# Create the ggplot object with updated aesthetics and faceting
# The plotting code remains largely the same, ggplot handles the reduced data
plot_vertical_dodged_filtered <- ggplot(
    df_long,
    # Define aesthetics:
    # x-axis: effect type ('coefficient_label': Baseline vs Combined)
    # y-axis: estimate value ('est')
    # color: distinguish lines/points based on condition ('condition')
    aes(x = coefficient_label, y = est, color = condition) # Uses the new 'condition' factor
  ) +
  # Add lines connecting the baseline point to the combined effect point for each condition within each facet
  geom_line(aes(group = condition), position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), linewidth = 1, alpha = 0.8) +
  # Add points representing the specific point estimates
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width), size = 3.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  # Add vertical error bars representing standard error (Estimate +/- SE)
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = est - se, ymax = est + se),
    position = position_dodge(width = dodge_width),
    width = 0.1, # Width of the error bar caps
    linewidth = 0.7
   ) +
  # Create separate panels (facets) for each outcome variable ('variable_short')
  facet_wrap(~ variable_short, ncol = 3) +
  # Add a horizontal dashed line at y=0 for reference
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", alpha = 0.8) +
  # Customize labels and title
  labs(
    title = "Impact of Treatment during Negative Shocks by Outcome Variable",
    subtitle = "Comparing Baseline (Shock Only) vs. Combined Effect (Shock + Treatment) - P50 Groups Only", # Updated subtitle
    x = "Effect Type",
    y = "Estimated Coefficient",
    color = "Condition"
  ) +
  # Apply a minimal theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  # Further theme customization
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 9, angle = 0, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")
  ) +
  # Use a colorblind-friendly color palette for the conditions
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Or "Dark2", "Paired"

# Display the plot
print(plot_vertical_dodged_filtered)

# --- Saving the Plot (Optional) ---
# Uncomment the following lines to save the plot to a file

# Save as PNG file
# ggsave("treatment_effect_vertical_dodged_filtered_plot.png", plot = plot_vertical_dodged_filtered, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Save as PDF file
# ggsave("treatment_effect_vertical_dodged_filtered_plot.pdf", plot = plot_vertical_dodged_filtered, width = 10, height = 6)

```
